First-promotion measurement for K8S-MISSING-LIMITS-01. Strong-single-corpus path per PROMOTION-DOCTRINE.md: 149 TP / 0 FP / 0 AMB on 149-finding clean denominator = 100.00% precision, 0.00% ambiguity, ≥30 clean denom — overwhelmingly above strong-single-corpus bar.

argo-cd manifests/ is the project's canonical install-manifest tree (install.yaml, core-install.yaml, HA variants, ApplicationSet, redis HA, namespace-install). Every YAML under manifests/ is intended for kubectl apply against a real cluster.

argo-cd ships unbounded by design — operators are expected to add resource constraints based on cluster sizing. This makes 100% of argo-cd's production manifests genuine TPs under the K8S-MISSING-LIMITS-01 predicate. The shape correctly identifies the production failure-mode risk: a cluster running argo-cd via raw kubectl apply will have unbounded controller resource consumption.
Determinism check passed: mismatch_count=0; two classifications produced byte-identical verdicts.

13 TPs include the same kubernetes monorepo test-artifact corpus-modeling gap documented in K8S-MISSING-LIMITS-01 v1: 12 of 13 TPs are kubectl/cli-runtime test artifacts under staging/src/k8s.io/<component>/artifacts/ paths the §2.4 production-context filter did not enumerate. 1 TP (staging/src/k8s.io/pod-security-admission/webhook/manifests/50-deployment.yaml) is a genuine production manifest.

Honest disclosure: the 52% precision benefits from the §2.4 corpus-modeling gap. If the gap were closed (test-artifact paths suppressed), the kubernetes corpus would either drop to ~5-10% precision or below the 10-floor on clean denom. The v1 ratification stands as recorded under the v1 rubric; v2 cycle would close the gap and re-measure. argo-cd 100% is the uncontested canonical signal of the shape's actual precision.

Honest-discipline preserved per METHODOLOGY-V2.md §3 frozen-rubric and §9 invalidation rules: no mid-run rubric expansion. The corpus-modeling gap is the same v2 candidate flagged in K8S-MISSING-LIMITS-01 v1 closeout — expand §2.4 to recognize monorepo test-artifact path conventions.

Third consecutive operator-deferred-decision shape to ratify at 100% on argo-cd: K8S-MISSING-LIMITS-01 (149/0/0), K8S-MISSING-PROBES-01 (41/0/0), K8S-MISSING-SECURITY-CONTEXT-01 (14/0/0). Lower TP count reflects more restrictive predicate (Pod-level inheritance walk catches some containers that would fire under simpler predicates).

14 TPs cover argocd-server, argocd-repo-server, argocd-application-controller, argocd-applicationset-controller, argocd-redis, argocd-dex-server, argocd-notifications-controller, argocd-commit-server across manifests/install.yaml, manifests/core-install.yaml, HA variants. argo-cd ships these without securityContext by design — operator is expected to apply Pod Security Standards or admission policies.

Predicate is multi-field, multi-level: detector walks Pod-level securityContext + container-level securityContext + handles container-level override semantics (container runAsNonRoot:false OR runAsUser:0 blocks Pod-level inheritance per Kubernetes semantics). Most structurally complex k8s detector to date; all 20 binding fixtures pass.
